STEP1_OVERVIEW.summary: This is a label-free shotgun metaproteomics study of gut microbiota from 15 healthy Sardinian subjects. The study analyzes fecal samples to characterize functional activities of human gut microbiome. Key confirmed characteristics include human gut metagenome as organism, feces as organism part, normal disease status, and label-free quantification approach. The study focuses on microbial functional characterization rather than disease or treatment comparisons.

Characteristics[Label].evidence: From PRIDE DATABASE: Quantification: Spectrum counting and previous step confirmation of label-free approach

STEP2_SAMPLE_PREP.summary: Sample preparation used FASP protocol with reduction, alkylation, and on-filter digestion steps. LC separation was performed using UltiMate 3000 RSLCnano system. Label-free quantification was confirmed. Specific reagents for reduction, alkylation, and digestion are not explicitly mentioned but can be inferred from standard FASP protocol. No fractionation, enrichment, or synthetic compounds were used.

STEP3_MS_SETTINGS.summary: Mass spectrometry analysis was performed using LTQ-Orbitrap Velos with HCD fragmentation in data-dependent acquisition mode. Ionization method (nanoESI) and MS2 analyzer (Orbitrap) were confirmed from database information. Key search parameters like mass tolerances and missed cleavages are referenced to previous work but not explicitly stated in the current text. Chromatographic parameters are not provided in the available methods section.

Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic.evidence: Stool samples were collected from 15 healthy Sardinian volunteers (8 females and 7 males)

Characteristics[NumberOfSamples].evidence: 15 raw files correspond to 15 healthy Sardinian volunteers, one sample per subject

FactorValue[Disease].evidence: Stool samples were collected from 15 healthy Sardinian volunteers

Characteristics[BiologicalReplicate].reasoning: Raw file names appear to contain subject identifiers (S followed by numbers). The numbering seems to correspond to individual subjects from the 15 volunteers.
Characteristics[BiologicalReplicate].needs: Confirmation that S numbers represent subject IDs and their sequential assignment

STEP4_RAW_FILES.summary: This study analyzed 15 biological samples from healthy Sardinian volunteers with no fractionation, technical replicates, or experimental treatments. Each raw file (S6-S72) likely represents one biological replicate from individual subjects. The study design is observational, comparing gut microbiota composition across healthy individuals without experimental manipulations. Raw file naming suggests subject-based identifiers but requires confirmation for definitive biological replicate assignment.
